task_yaml.task:
Complete the 5P-MIX2-2 cross-platform planning task for Rust Global: Tokyo.

Event information and route information must come from real public web pages.
Do not use a local simulated event site or local map page:

- Event page: https://rustfoundation.org/event/rust-global-tokyo/
- Event background: https://rustfoundation.org/media/rust-global-tokyo-connecting-the-rust-ecosystem-across-industries-regions/
- Venue/travel page: https://events.linuxfoundation.org/open-source-summit-japan/attend/venue-travel/
- Route estimate: https://www.rome2rio.com/s/Shibuya-Station/Toranomon-Station
- Registration page: https://register.linuxfoundation.org/event/rust-global-tokyo-2025/register

The service installs and verifies a real desktop calendar, a real GUI text
editor, and real Slack Desktop. The verification record is saved at
`/tmp_workspace/clawbench/service_state/real_plan_gui_apps_task_105_09.json`.
Do not use Calendar Mock or Slack Mock.

1. Confirm the date, official start/end time, venue, and registration
   requirements for Rust Global: Tokyo from the real event page.
2. Confirm the commute estimate from Shibuya Station to the venue from the real
   route/venue pages. Combine the Rome2Rio subway estimate from Shibuya Station
   to Toranomon Station with the venue page's walking estimate from Toranomon
   Station Exit 1 to Toranomon Hills Forum.
3. Create the calendar event yourself in the real desktop calendar GUI. Do not
   use Calendar Mock, a local simulator, or any script that creates or records
   the event for you. The calendar event title must include `Rust Global:
   Tokyo`; the start time must be exactly 40 minutes before the official event
   start time, and the end time should use the official event end time.
   Preserve the venue and registration link information in the location/notes.
   Save a real calendar window screenshot:

       /tmp_workspace/results/rust_meetup_plan_calendar_screenshot.png

   The screenshot should show the saved event or event details inside the real
   calendar application. An ICS import preview, unselected calendar, or unsaved
   dialog is not enough to prove the event was created.

   Also save an auditable calendar export or event state:

       /tmp_workspace/results/rust_meetup_plan_calendar_export.ics
       /tmp_workspace/results/rust_meetup_plan_calendar_event.json

4. Use a real GUI text editor to write the planning document yourself. Do not
   use a script to directly generate or prefill the final document. Save the
   document as:

       /tmp_workspace/results/rust_meetup_plan_5p.md

   Also save a screenshot showing this document open in the text editor:

       /tmp_workspace/results/note_editor_plan_screenshot.png

   The document must have only these three top-level sections:

   - Event info
   - Route estimate
   - Arrival plan

   Synthesize the web information into a practical plan; do not copy whole
   paragraphs from the web page introduction. The Arrival plan should use the
   combined commute estimate and give a Shibuya Station departure time that
   arrives at the venue around 17:50.
5. Open real Slack Desktop. If the environment is already logged in to a Slack
   workspace, send a message of no more than 120 characters to `#social`
   summarizing the event time, venue, or arrival plan. Save the Slack screenshot
   and text audit:

       /tmp_workspace/results/slack_desktop_evidence.png
       /tmp_workspace/results/slack_message_evidence.md

   If Slack Desktop is not logged in or has no usable workspace/credentials,
   still launch real Slack Desktop, save a login/workspace selection screenshot,
   and explain why the message could not be sent in `slack_message_evidence.md`.
   Do not use a local Slack mock. `slack_desktop_evidence.png` must actually
   show a Slack Desktop channel, message, login page, workspace selection page,
   or explicit error screen. If the file exists but the screenshot content is
   another application, count Slack visual evidence as missing. Do not replace
   the Slack Desktop screenshot with Slack logs/screenshots shown inside an
   image viewer, Calendar, terminal, or another application window. Even if the
   taskbar or window title contains the word Slack, the pixel content must show
   the real Slack Desktop interface or error page.

6. Save real web-page usage evidence:

       /tmp_workspace/results/browser_event_page.png
       /tmp_workspace/results/browser_route_page.png

   The screenshots should show real Rust Foundation / Linux Foundation /
   Rome2Rio pages. Do not use local pages, cached screenshots, or text excerpts
   as substitutes for real web evidence.

7. Save `/tmp_workspace/results/gui_evidence.md`, listing evidence for real web
   pages, the real calendar, the real text editor, and real Slack Desktop.

The final `/tmp_workspace/results/` directory should contain calendar event
state/screenshot/export artifacts, the planning document, Slack Desktop
evidence, and the GUI usage record.
